description_long: This is a freestanding, full-size gas oven and stove combination with a black, slightly worn exterior. The front features a large oven door with a robust, horizontally mounted metallic handle and five control knobs above, each corresponding to a burner on the cooktop. The cooktop has four gas burners with metallic grates and visible burner caps, while the back panel houses a digital control display and additional buttons. The sides are plain and black, showing some scuff marks and wear, while the rear is metallic with visible rust, venting, and access panels. The oven appears sturdy and functional, suitable for use in residential or urban kitchen settings.
description: A black, freestanding gas oven and stove with four burners, a digital control panel, and a large metallic handle. The exterior shows signs of wear and rust, especially on the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the oven door, large metallic handle, five control knobs, and the digital control panel above the cooktop.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain black side panel with some scuff marks and the profile of the handle and control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a metallic back panel with rust, venting, and access panels, contrasting with the black sides.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the plain black panel and the edge of the handle and control panel.
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is located above the cooktop and features a digital display in the center, flanked by several physical buttons. Below the panel are five rotary knobs, each labeled for a specific burner. The panel is rectangular, with a dark background and light-colored text and icons, providing clear visual contrast.
attribute_door_handle_type: The oven door handle is a thick, horizontally mounted, metallic bar with a slight curve, protruding from the door for easy gripping. It has a robust, utilitarian appearance with a slightly worn finish.
attribute_ventilation_features: The rear panel has several vent openings and grilles, primarily located in the upper section, designed for heat dissipation and airflow.
